skill.name: competitor-analysis
skill.description: Analyze competitor websites, page structure, messaging, trust signals, and feature gaps for content and market strategy.
skill.summary: Review competitor websites and turn page observations into structured strategic recommendations.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives in documentation text and a fenced output example. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it explicitly enables browser navigation and external web search for competitor research.

medium_findings.title: External Web Research Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill allows browser navigation and external web search tools. This is aligned with competitor research, but users should avoid sending confidential strategy, customer data, or private target lists through web search workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools explicitly include browser navigation and web search. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates a real data exposure consideration.

low_findings.title: Documentation Text Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The detected backtick usage marks an inline source file path, a table name, and a fenced JSON output example. No executable shell command or user-controlled command path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown documentation and an example output block. There is no Ruby code, shell invocation, or execution instruction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported lines contain the skill description and a normal usage heading. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing implementation, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain descriptive prose only. The scanner likely matched a short substring inside ordinary English text.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URL in Output Example
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL appears inside a fenced JSON output example as a placeholder competitor website. It is not a request destination or embedded tracking endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is part of a documented output schema and uses a generic example domain. No code path sends data to this URL.

content.value_statement: Competitive website research is slow when each page is reviewed without a clear framework. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to compare structure, messaging, trust signals, and feature gaps.
actual_capabilities: Defines a page-by-page framework for homepage, about, services, pricing, contact, and resource pages.
Extracts website sections, headlines, calls to action, trust signals, and unique feature patterns.
Ranks competitors as national leaders, regional leaders, local competitors, or emerging entrants.
Identifies missing features, missing trust signals, messaging gaps, and prioritized recommendations.
Encourages dated analysis so users can track changes in competitor websites over time.
limitations: Requires web access or user-provided page content to analyze live competitor websites.
Does not verify private revenue, traffic, or market share data unless the user provides reliable sources.
Can describe messaging patterns, but users remain responsible for avoiding plagiarism.
The documented JSON output is a format guide, not an automated database integration.

use_cases.title: Plan a Website Redesign
use_cases.description: Compare competitor page structures before changing navigation, homepage sections, or conversion paths.

use_cases.title: Enter a New Local Market
use_cases.description: Review regional and local competitors to identify trust signals, offers, and messaging standards.

use_cases.title: Improve Service Pages
use_cases.description: Find missing proof points, calls to action, and content sections across direct competitor pages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Competitor Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one competitor site.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this competitor website: [URL]. Review the homepage, services page, pricing page, and contact page. Summarize structure, messaging, CTAs, trust signals, and unique features.

prompt_templates.title: Local Market Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several competitors in one market.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these local competitors: [URLs]. Rank them by competitor type, then identify repeated page patterns, missing trust signals, messaging gaps, and practical recommendations for our website.

prompt_templates.title: Conversion Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus on lead generation and conversion paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these competitor pages for conversion strategy: [URLs]. Compare CTA text, form fields, offer placement, urgency language, proof points, and follow-up recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Quarterly Competitive Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dated quarterly competitor analysis for [market or segment]. Include national leaders, regional competitors, local competitors, emerging entrants, strategic changes, risks, opportunities, and prioritized actions.

output_examples.input: Analyze three cleaning service competitors before a homepage redesign.
output_examples.output: A structured brief with competitor rankings, homepage section maps, common headline themes, CTA patterns, trust signals, unique features, and prioritized redesign recommendations.

output_examples.input: Compare local competitors before launching in a new city.
output_examples.output: A market entry summary showing local positioning, proof points, service page patterns, pricing presentation, missing credibility markers, and launch content opportunities.

output_examples.input: Review competitor contact pages for conversion gaps.
output_examples.output: A conversion-focused comparison of form length, CTA language, phone visibility, urgency cues, guarantees, and recommended contact page improvements.

best_practices: Provide competitor URLs, your market, and your own website context before asking for recommendations.
Ask for dated findings because competitor websites and offers can change quickly.
Use the analysis to identify patterns and gaps, not to copy competitor wording.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential customer lists or private strategy documents to web search workflows.
Do not treat competitor claims as verified facts without checking independent sources.
Do not request copied headlines, page text, or proprietary competitor content.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes competitor website structure, messaging, calls to action, trust signals, unique features, and strategic gaps.

faq.question: Can it browse live websites?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for browser navigation and web search tools when those tools are available.

faq.question: Does it produce verified market share data?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on observable website patterns unless the user provides reliable market data.

faq.question: Is the output limited to cleaning businesses?
faq.answer: No. The framework can apply to many service businesses, though the source project appears cleaning-focused.

faq.question: Can it recommend website changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It turns competitor observations into prioritized recommendations for features, proof points, messaging, and page structure.

faq.question: What should users avoid sharing?
faq.answer: Users should avoid sharing private customer data, confidential strategy, or sensitive target lists during web research.
